content.text:
Looking at the image, the track runs straight ahead with the dashed centerline visible, and the car appears to be on course heading eastward along the straight.

**Waypoint Analysis:**
- nearest_waypoints[1] = 111, target = (111 + 5) % 121 = 116
- Target WP116: x=5.4559, y=0.6841
- Car: x=2.0213, y=0.8062, heading=-22.43°
- dy = 0.6841 - 0.8062 = -0.1221
- dx = 5.4559 - 2.0213 = 3.4346
- bearing = atan2(-0.1221, 3.4346) = -2.04°
- delta = -2.04° - (-22.43°) = +20.39°
- Positive delta → waypoint is to the LEFT → steer LEFT (positive steering)

Delta ~20.4°, still needs left correction but decreasing (was 23.6°). Car heading improving steadily. Use moderate left steer.

```json
{
  "reasoning": "Track runs straight east ahead with car heading -22.4° and delta=+20.4° still requiring left correction to align with the eastward centerline.",
  "knowledge": [
    "Positive steering = left turn (CCW heading increase), negative = right turn (CW)",
    "Track centerline y~0.68 at this straight section, car y~0.81 — slightly north of center",
    "Car heading steadily correcting: -43.96 → -30.52 → -25.60 → -22.43, continue left steer",
    "Track runs nearly due east for next ~20 waypoints (y stays ~0.68)",
    "After heading aligns with track (~-2°), reduce steering quickly to avoid overshoot",
    "Track width 0.76m — very narrow, precision required",
    "Once delta < 10°, switch to 0 or minimal steering to avoid oscillation",
    "Progress 94.0% — nearly complete lap, stay on track at all costs",
    "Car is left of center — heading correction will naturally bring it toward center",
    "Speed 0.75 appropriate while large heading correction still needed"
  ],
  "movement_analysis": {
    "x": [1.871, 1.911, 1.968, 2.021],
    "y": [0.818, 0.811, 0.807, 0.806],
    "yaw": [-34.23, -30.52, -25.60, -22.43]
  },
  "waypoint_analysis": "Target WP116 at (5.456, 0.684): dy=-0.122, dx=3.435, bearing=-2.04°, delta=+20.4° → LEFT steer needed",
  "speed": 0.75,
  "steering_angle": 15.0
}
```
